Requirement Statement: Platform must evolve from Google tools/Python scripts into a professional web platform for wedding guest operations.
System must serve Diginoces internal operations first while remaining SaaS-ready for partner scaling.
Diginoces must control pricing, branding, contracts, packages, and client experience.
Version 1 must be WhatsApp-first for guest/client communication.
Canva remains the design/export tool; the app supplies structured data and personalization.
Future app must use app-owned file storage rather than Google Drive as the operational storage system.
System must support global roles, project-level roles, event-level roles, and custom roles.
Diginoces admin must have full platform control including settings, users, pricing, payments, contracts, reports, and audit logs.
Diginoces staff access must depend on assignment and permissions, not automatic full access.
External partners can create/manage assigned projects but cannot manage pricing, see revenue, approve payment exceptions, or access internal reports.
Bride and groom can edit their own side while unlocked and view the partner side, but cannot edit partner list directly.
Check-in staff must have full accounts and only access check-in operations for assigned events.
Sensitive roles must require two-factor authentication.
Internal notes must be visible only to Diginoces/admin and authorized staff and editable only within 15 minutes.
Guests must access only their own secure public page without creating accounts.
System hierarchy must be Wedding Project → Events/Celebrations → Event-specific operations.
One wedding project must support multiple events/celebrations.
Each project must have an automatically generated unique readable project code.
Event codes must be auto-generated from event type and editable by Diginoces/admin.
Each project must have one master guest database, with guests assigned to one or more events.
Each event inside a project can have its own package/add-ons while contract remains project-level.
Each project must have an automatically generated workflow checklist based on project/event setup.
System must maintain one master guest database per wedding project.
System must preserve separate bride and groom working lists backed by the master guest database.
Users with permission must be able to manually add guests through the app.
System must support CSV/Excel guest import with column mapping and preview.
Bride/groom imports must require Diginoces/admin review before guests become active.
System must validate required fields before invitation generation and key workflows.
Diginoces/admin and bride/groom users must be able to create guest title/types with count safeguards.
System must detect possible duplicate guests across bride/groom lists and imports.
A guest can belong to both bride and groom sides while counted only once.
System must support household/family grouping while keeping separate member invitations/check-ins.
System must support couple-created tags and internal Diginoces tags.
System must support bulk editing of guests with permission and lock-stage controls.
Guest lists must lock after invitation generation/sending, routing couple changes through requests.
After lock bride/groom must submit structured change requests for guest changes.
Printed-only guests can lack WhatsApp number with admin override, use manual RSVP and manual check-in.
Each guest must have a secure personal public page accessible without account creation.
Guest public page must remain locked until full payment or payment exception override.
Diginoces/admin can preview guest public pages before payment unlock; couple cannot.
Guest page must have elegant wedding-style design with couple photo and event information.
Guest page must support French and English with guest preferred language.
RSVP options must be Yes, No, and Maybe.
Guest invited to multiple events uses one RSVP flow with answers stored per event.
Each event must have its own RSVP deadline.
Guest can change RSVP only if previous answer was Maybe; Yes/No lock after submission.
RSVP No excludes guest from expected attendance, table capacity, reminders, and check-in; Maybe remains included.
Maybe guests must receive customizable follow-up reminders before RSVP deadline.
Pending/Maybe after deadline must enter manual review by Diginoces/admin and couple.
Guests must be able to download their own latest invitation PDF from their public page.
Printed-only guest RSVP must be handled manually by Diginoces/couple.
Canva remains the design tool; app imports Canva-exported PDF templates.
Invitation templates must be managed at event level.
Diginoces/admin can upload Canva PDF templates and track template status.
App must provide a visual drag-and-drop editor for dynamic field placement.
Templates must support dynamic guest, table, QR, event, couple, and invitation fields.
PDF generator must support smart text fitting for long names and titles.
Public guest page QR and check-in QR must remain separate.
QR codes must use secure short tokens instead of long WhatsApp URLs.
Full invitation generation must be blocked until Diginoces/admin approves technical preview.
Couple design approval remains manual outside app but must be recordable in the app.
System must support invitation generation by event, selected guests, table, status, and all.
System must block invitation generation for guests missing required or conditional data.
System must track invitation statuses including generated, sent, needs regeneration, failed, resent, cancelled.
Relevant data changes must mark invitations as needing regeneration.
Regenerated invitations must keep version history and expose only the latest version to guests.
Version 1 must be WhatsApp-first and support API mode plus guided manual mode.
System must use approved templates for invitation, RSVP, reminders, modifications, welcome, and resend messages.
System must use approved French/English templates selected by guest preferred language.
System must block invitation sending until full payment or approved exception and required data are present.
System must prepare/send Maybe follow-up reminders according to event schedule.
System must detect changes that may require modification messages and let staff decide action.
Check-in should send/prepare welcome/table message on first arrival only for Couple invitations.
System must track message statuses including prepared, queued, opened manually, sent, failed, skipped, resent.
Guided manual sending must record staff member, time, guest, event, and status.
Core product must avoid unofficial WhatsApp Web automation as primary method.
Check-in only by logged-in assigned staff with full accounts.
Check-in must support QR scan, invitation ID search, name search, phone search, table search, and side filters.
QR check-in must be event-specific and not mark attendance for other events.
Check-in screen must be mobile/tablet optimized with large buttons and clear guest/table data.
Couple invitations must support 0/2, 1/2, and 2/2 arrival states.
For Couple invitations second arrival updates count only and does not send another welcome message.
Household/family groups must be checked in member by member.
Unexpected guests must require supervisor approval before entry.
Unexpected guest approval must support in-app approval and manual approval recording.
VIP/protocol guests must be visually highlighted on check-in screen without separate alert in v1.
System must support preloaded offline guest list and sync when online.
Printed-only guests must be checked in manually by name/search.
System must support assignment of check-in devices/stations to staff/events.
Dashboard must show real-time arrivals by table and by staff/device.
Tables and seating must be managed separately per event.
System must support table-level, seat-level, and mixed assignment modes per event.
Each table must have structured fields including ID, name, description, capacity, event, status, and display order.
System must support individual and bulk table creation.
System must calculate occupancy and warn on full/over-capacity tables.
System must support list/table view and visual drag-and-drop seating map.
System must clearly show invited guests not assigned to a table.
After invitations are sent couple table changes must become change requests.
RSVP No excludes from active seating; Maybe remains included.
System must support VIP/protocol tags and special seating notes.
System must generate Canva Bulk Create CSV for table cards.
System must track printed invitation workflow statuses.
System must generate one contract for the whole wedding project.
Contract must be displayed in-app and approved with checkbox confirmation.
Only one couple member must approve contract; groom is preferred approver.
Version 1 does not include contract negotiation/change request flow inside app.
System must support addendums for price/scope changes such as added guests/events/services.
System must support Diginoces-admin-managed service packages and add-ons.
Each event can have its own selected package/add-ons while project has one contract.
Version 1 pricing must use USD only with all-inclusive amounts and no tax breakdown.
Pricing must be based on planned guest count.
Planned guest count increase after contract approval must trigger additional amount/addendum workflow.
Planned guest count decrease after contract approval does not automatically reduce price.
Diginoces/admin can manually apply commercial discount/gesture with reason and audit trail.
Payments are handled outside app but manually recorded in app.
Full payment unlocks invitation sending and guest public page access.
Diginoces/admin can approve exception override before full payment with reason and audit.
Guests submit wishes through their public guest page, replacing Google Forms.
Version 1 supports text wishes with emojis only; no audio/video/photo uploads.
Each guest can submit only one wish and edit same wish before deadline.
Diginoces/admin can review, edit, approve, exclude, and moderate wishes.
System must preserve original wish and separate approved/edited wish for printing.
Couple can review and approve wishes before guest-book export.
System must export approved wishes as Canva Bulk Create CSV.
Final guest-book CSV/PDF files must be stored in project file library.
External planners/providers are partners who bring weddings to Diginoces under Diginoces brand/pricing.
Each partner must have an account with status, contact info, project links, and permissions.
Partners can create draft wedding projects and submit them for Diginoces/admin approval.
Partner-created projects must be approved by Diginoces/admin before couple access.
Partners cannot manage pricing, see revenue, approve payment exceptions, or view internal notes/audit logs.
Partners can communicate with couple through project comment thread; Diginoces/admin can see it.
Version 1 tracks projects brought by partners but does not manage commissions/referral fees.
System must provide global, project, event, couple, partner, check-in, and operations dashboards with role-based visibility.
Global business dashboard visible only to Diginoces/admin and authorized internal roles.
Couple dashboard shows simplified progress without internal notes/audit/revenue.
Partner dashboard shows assigned/originated projects without revenue/payment details.
Reports must export as PDF, Excel, or CSV depending on use case and permissions.
System must keep internal, read-only audit logs for sensitive actions.
Audit logs visible only to Diginoces/admin and authorized internal roles.
Future platform must use own internal file storage as official storage system.
Each wedding project must have a file library with categories.
System must support event-level files such as templates, reports, table CSVs, and check-in reports.
System must support guest-specific files linked to guest, event, invitation ID, version, and active status.
Key files must support version history with active/latest marker.
Guest-facing file downloads must use secure, revocable links and expose only own files.
Project files retained for one year after wedding before deletion/archive action.
System must store Canva CSV exports with metadata and versioning.
File uploads must validate type, size, corruption, and dangerous formats.
AI assistance must suggest, draft, clean, summarize, and warn without bypassing user permissions.
AI available to admin, staff, partners, bride, groom with permission-aware behavior.
Bride/groom can apply AI suggestions directly only while list is unlocked; after lock suggestions become requests.
AI should support guest name cleanup, duplicate detection, phone formatting, import assistance, tags, seating suggestions.
AI can draft WhatsApp messages and translations but approved templates control sending.
AI-accepted data changes must be logged with human user as actor.
Version 1 must be a responsive web app optimized for phones, tablets, laptops.
Recommended stack: React/Next.js + TypeScript frontend PostgreSQL database modular backend.
A relational PostgreSQL database is recommended for structured project/event/guest data.
Backend must enforce role-based permissions, not only frontend restrictions.
Heavy/scheduled tasks must use background jobs/queues.
PDF generation should run in background worker or dedicated service.
Offline check-in should use PWA + IndexedDB/preload/sync design.
App should integrate with Google Calendar while internal scheduling remains source of truth.
Version 1 uses Canva CSV export; direct Canva integration can be explored later.
Public guest tokens and check-in tokens must be secure, separate, and revocable.
Project must be built progressively by phases rather than all at once.
MVP must include core end-to-end wedding operation features before advanced AI/integrations.
Native mobile app, direct Canva integration, full WhatsApp API automation, commissions, multi-currency, white-label SaaS are post-MVP.
